t_ch: 有些人就算得到宝剑，难免要落入旁人之手。
t_en: Yeah, and even if someone gets their hands on such a precious sword, it'll probably end up in someone else's.

t_ch: 今日在丹鼎司的胜负还没分出，云璃小姐既然有兴趣，不如代表朱明仙舟上台一战？
t_en: The outcome of our duel at the Alchemy Commission is still up in the air. Since you're interested, why don't you represent the Xianzhou Zhuming and challenge me in the ring?

t_ch: 我正有此意！胜者花落谁家还不一定呢！也许是我，也许是别人，也许还是我，但总之…未必是他！
t_en: That's exactly what I had in mind! Nobody knows who's gonna come out on top! It could be me, could be someone else... It'll probably be me, but whatever happens... it won't be him!

t_ch: 看来真被飞霄说中了。
t_en: Looks like Feixiao was right.

t_ch: 他俩碰上，非死即伤…
t_en: It'll be either death or disaster when these two clash...

t_ch: 我不想说的太失礼…但是，这场连续剧看了那么久，我还真想瞧瞧，他们俩到底谁胜谁负。
t_en: Not to be rude or anything... We've been watching their drama. I'm dying to find out who beats who.

t_ch: 在客人面前都给我消停消停！老朽送出宝剑，是想为仪典增光添彩，不是想听你们彼此争吵，有伤盟谊。
t_en: Quiet down! We have other guests here! I've prepared this sword to add some excitement to the ceremony, not to have you two squabble. It's not a good look for the Alliance.

t_ch: 虽然你们俩都有不会输给对方的自信。但擂台之事总是一胜一负，难免伤了和气。老朽有个想法——
t_en: While you both seem confident that you'll win, you need to remember there can only be one winner and one loser in the ring, which could lead to hard feelings. Actually, I have an idea...

t_ch: 虽说胜者尚未揭晓，也未必就是你二人，但若想得到这彩头，我要你们联手教出一名学徒，让他登上演武仪典，至少赢下一场胜利。
t_en: We don't know who the winner will be, and it might not be either of you, but if you're eyeing that prize, you'll need to work together. I want you to take on an apprentice who will take part in the Wardance and win at least one match.

t_ch: 哪有这样的道理？
t_en: How does that make sense?

t_ch: 依老朽愚见，云骑剑士卓然于胜利之巅固然可喜；但能传下技艺，令其开枝散叶，嘉惠旁人，才更有价值。
t_en: In my humble opinion, while a Cloud Knight is commendable by securing victories, it's even more so to pass on your skills and spread the way of swordplay.

t_ch: 若是此人能以云骑剑术代表列车参加演武仪典，大展身手，老朽也会大感快慰！
t_en: I'd be greatly pleased if this apprentice could represent the Express in the Wardance by displaying their Cloud Knight flair and prowess.

t_ch: 炎老这想法着实有趣。传徒授艺，方显合作精神。如此一来，胜者有所斩获，败者也不至于空手而归。只是，他们二人该收谁为徒呢？
t_en: Well, Elder Huaiyan's idea is quite interesting. Imparting swordplay skills requires teamwork, and both the winner and the loser will learn a valuable lesson regardless of the outcome. The question is, whom should the two of them take as an apprentice?

t_ch: 嗯？谁？我？我吗？
t_en: Huh? Who? Wait, me...?

t_ch: 方才我看这位三月小姐对你们俩擂台剑斗之事颇为上心。不如就由你们教导她剑术入门。
t_en: I noticed just now that Miss March seemed quite interested in the outcome of your sword fight, so I thought why not teach her the art of swordplay?

t_ch: …哎？欸！老将军不会是认真的吧？
t_en: ...Huh? Wait! Are you serious, General?

t_ch: 怎么突然把我给牵扯进来了？我也没练过剑啊！零基础啊！学得会吗！不会到最后耻辱地被逐出师门吧！
t_en: Why am I being dragged into this all of a sudden? I've never practiced swordplay before! I'm a total newbie! You really think I can learn it? You'll probably realize I have no hope and give up on me, and that'd be sooo embarrassing!

t_ch: 为什么不选丹恒？
t_en: Why don't you choose Dan Heng?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，事情已经够麻烦的了。
t_en: Things are already complicated enough as they are, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 震鳞赫赫，击云掣电…以丹恒先生的身手，还需要别人指教吗？
t_en: He strikes like lightning... Does Mr. Dan Heng really need anyone's guidance?

t_ch: 倒是三月小姐…仍是块未开凿的璞玉。
t_en: As for Miss March... she's like a piece of jade in the rough, just waiting to be shaped.

t_ch: 为什么不选我？
t_en: Why don't you choose me?

t_ch: 这位小朋友的筋骨脉络倒是…颇为别致。你也对剑术有兴趣吗？
t_en: Your talent is quite... unique. Are you also interested in swordplay?

t_ch: 不过依老朽多年看人的眼光，阁下既无需钻研剑艺裨补实力…这世上只怕也没什么人能为你指点前路。
t_en: But you know, based on my years of judging people, you don't really need to dive into swordplay to boost your skills... and I doubt there's anyone out there who can really show you the way forward.

t_ch: 倒是三月小姐…仍是块未开凿的璞玉。
t_en: As for Miss March... she's like a piece of jade in the rough, just waiting to be shaped.

t_ch: 三月，你还是从了吧！
t_en: Just accept your fate, March!

t_ch: 这可是个好机会？我记得你提过自己想学什么剑招来着？
t_en: Isn't this a perfect chance for you? I remember you mentioning that you wanted to learn some sword moves.

t_ch: 呃，好像是有这么回事。但这事情太突然了吧？
t_en: Yeah, I did say that, but this is all happening so quickly, don't you think?

t_ch: 三月小姐仍是块未开凿的璞玉，老朽也好奇，想借罗浮演武仪典的良机。
t_en: Miss March is like a piece of jade in the rough, just waiting to be shaped. The Wardance is the perfect opportunity to see what heights she can reach.

t_ch: 谢谢怀炎将军的厚爱。云璃和彦卿原本是要决一胜负，如今却要转头教我学剑，岂不是太耽误两位练剑备战的时间了？
t_en: I appreciate your kind words, General Huaiyan. But won't teaching me swordplay be a waste of Yanqing and Yunli's time? They should be preparing for the ceremony.

t_ch: 何况，听说剑士们都各有绝招呀，要是教我的时候不小心泄漏，大家知根知底，打起来岂不是破不了招？
t_en: Plus, I heard that each swordmaster has their own special moves. What if they let something slip while teaching me? If everyone knows each other's tactics, won't that mean everyone is stuck in a stalemate and unable to end their fights during the Wardance?

t_ch: 三月七你倒是个体贴人。放心吧，不勤修苦练个十来年还教不到绝招呢。你别害怕，以朱明剑术的厉害，只要跟我学上几招，包你受用无穷。
t_en: That's considerate of you, March 7th. But don't worry. It'll take you at least a decade of hard training before you can even start learning special moves. No need to freak out – a few Zhuming swordplay tricks will mean you'll be more than equipped.

t_ch: 真、真的吗？
t_en: R—really?

t_ch: 看来三月是完全心动了啊。
t_en: Looks like March's curiosity has been piqued.

t_ch: 呵呵，要的就是知根知底。演武台上一招制敌，那多没劲啊！何况决定剑士生死的，可不是什么「绝招」，而是扎实的基本功。
t_en: Heh, the whole point is to know each other's moves. Defeating your opponent in just one move? How boring would that be! Plus, what really decides a swordsman's fate isn't some "special move" — it's the solid fundamentals.

t_ch: 这么说来，云璃小姐是应承了。彦卿，你觉得呢？
t_en: So, Miss Yunli has already agreed. What do you say, Yanqing?

t_ch: 将军…彦卿尚未出师，哪里来的资格传授他人剑术。
t_en: General... I haven't graduated yet. How can I be qualified to teach swordplay to others?

t_ch: 那就是认输咯？好啊，既然你连教人的自信都没有，不如我来替你守罗浮的擂台如何？
t_en: So, you're admitting your defeat, huh? If you're not even confident to teach, why don't you let me be the ringmaster instead?

t_ch: 彦卿，传徒授艺的过程也是在审视自己所学，加深对技艺的理解。你做了多年弟子，是时候该换换眼界了。
t_en: Yanqing, teaching an apprentice is also a way of honing your own skills and gaining insights. You've been an apprentice for years, it's about time you looked at swordplay from another perspective.

t_ch: 我明白啦，就依将军所说，我答应了！
t_en: I see, General. Then count me in!

t_ch: 既然彦卿也答应了，那么剩下的便只有三月小姐一个点头。
t_en: Now that Yanqing has agreed too, it all comes down to Miss March giving her nod.

t_ch: 三月，这件事归根结底要由你自己来决定。
t_en: It's up to you to make the final decision, March.

t_ch: 转职成为列车第一剑客吧。
t_en: Time for you to become the best swordmaster on the Express.

t_ch: 这样一来，我就不用担心你每次射中我屁股了。
t_en: At least that way, I won't have to worry about you accidentally shooting me in the butt all the time.

t_ch: 我一向射得很准！
t_en: Hey, I've never missed my target!

t_ch: 这可不是一条简单的路啊。
t_en: It won't be a walk in the park, that's for sure.

t_ch: 唔，虽然咱也算得上聪明伶俐，但学剑，听起来辛苦的很。
t_en: Yeah, while I'm smart, learning swordplay sounds like a whole lot of work.

t_ch: 你学剑也好，这样我就不用担心你每次射中我屁股了。
t_en: It'll be great learning swordplay. That way, I won't have to worry about you accidentally shooting me in the butt all the time.

t_ch: 我一向射得很准！
t_en: Hey, I've never missed my target!

t_ch: 还是得你来做决定。
t_en: It's all up to you, though.

t_ch: 我明白了。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 那就这么定了！谢谢怀炎将军的厚爱。
t_en: Then I'm on board! Thank you for your kindness, General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 好哇！自明日开始，彦卿与云璃会每日教你云骑剑术的基本要旨。
t_en: Great! Starting tomorrow, Yanqing and Yunli will teach you the basics of the Cloud Knights' swordplay.

t_ch: 接下来我和云璃先走一步，去为三月小姐置办些练剑的行头，权当是拜师礼了～
t_en: Yunli and I will head out and purchase some sword practice equipment for Miss March. Think of it as a little initiation gift.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，您太客气了…等等，谁给谁准备拜师礼？
t_en: Ahaha, you're too kind... Wait, you're giving me a gift? Shouldn't it be the other way round?

t_ch: 怀炎笑而不语，随即带着云璃离开了神策府。
t_en: Huaiyan, smiling silently, soon departs Seat of Divine Foresight with Yunli in tow.

t_ch: 哪有这样的道理？
t_en: How does that make sense?

t_ch: 呃，讨论是不是跑题了？事情怎么会变成这样？
t_en: Uh... I think we strayed off topic. How did things even get to this point?

t_ch: 是啊，我将诸位带来神策府，是因为「将军有要事商讨」，怎么会…怎么会一眨眼的功夫，我和云璃就成了三月小姐的剑术师父？
t_en: Yeah, I brought you here because the general said he had some important matters to discuss. But how in the world... did Yunli and I suddenly become Miss March's swordplay mentors?

t_ch: 因为…怀炎先生希望我们在罗浮仙舟驻留一段时日。
t_en: Because... General Huaiyan wants us to stick around on the Luofu for some time.

t_ch: 可我们本就是来观礼的？
t_en: But aren't we here to attend the ceremony, anyway?

t_ch: 但在他看来，我们与那些随时会离开的游客并无区别。
t_en: But from his point of view, we're no different from all the other tourists who may leave at any time.

t_ch: 罗浮上呈联盟的报告中既然出现了列车的行动记录，他一定想眼见为实，看看我们是否真有报告提及的那般能耐，而不是虚应故事找来的借口。
t_en: Since the Crew's actions were mentioned in the Luofu's operations log that was given to the Alliance, he probably wants to see firsthand if we're as capable as the report claims, or if we're just some made-up excuse to save face.

t_ch: 演武仪典就是他的试金石。为此，他甚至将云璃也一块儿拉下了水。原本只是两个年轻人之间的竞技赌斗…现在却成了你俩联手教导三月七。
t_en: And he wants to see it for himself during the Wardance, which is why he even dragged Yunli into this. What began as a simple contest between two swordmasters... has now evolved into you two collaborating to mentor March.

t_ch: 炎老还是那个令所有人为之头疼的炎老啊。
t_en: Elder Huaiyan is still that tricky general who likes to give everyone a headache.

t_ch: 我要在此致歉。我邀请诸位返航观礼，是存了向两位将军出示人证的心思，但没能坦诚相告，是景元的不是。
t_en: My apologies. Truth be told, I invited all of you to the ceremony because I wished for you to act as my witnesses. I apologize for not disclosing this information earlier.

t_ch: 除去炎老之外，在之后的几周内，我还会邀请各位同飞霄将军见上一面，接受她的提问以解其疑惑，希望各位到时候有所准备。
t_en: In the coming weeks, I'll also invite all of you to a meeting with General Feixiao, where you may need to answer her questions and clear up any doubts she might have. So, please be prepared for the meeting.

t_ch: 客气了，几句问话而已。
t_en: No worries. Answering a few questions is no big deal.

t_ch: 将军不必介怀。无论如何，我都会留下作为列车的人证，接受询问。
t_en: Don't worry, General. No matter what happens, I'm prepared to stay here as the Express' witness, and answer any questions.

t_ch: 那咱们连夜打包走人？
t_en: Perhaps we should just pack up and leave tonight?

t_ch: 咱们是做错什么了吗？咱们确确实实拯救了罗浮，为什么要像个逃犯一样走人呢？
t_en: Did we do something wrong? We really did save the Luofu, so why do you want to sneak off like we're fugitives?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，不必担忧。无论如何，我会留下作为列车的人证，接受询问。
t_en: Don't worry, {NICKNAME}. No matter what happens, I'll stay here as a witness and answer any questions she might have.

t_ch: 谢谢你们。
t_en: Thank you, everyone.

t_ch: 将军！彦卿能为将军分担的不多，但彦卿……
t_en: General! I know there isn't much I can do to share your burden, but...

t_ch: 嗯？
t_en: Hm?

t_ch: 彦卿一定会为罗浮守住演武仪典的擂台！
t_en: As the Luofu ringmaster, I won't let anyone defeat me in the Wardance!

t_ch: 我知道。
t_en: I know.

t_ch: 时候也不早了…
t_en: It's getting late...

t_ch: 炎老再稍留片刻
t_en: Please, stay a little longer, Elder Huaiyan.

t_ch: 我请来了列车的客人
t_en: I've invited some guests from the Express.

t_ch: 啊…怀炎将军走了。我怎么觉得刚才的话题有些不对劲啊！
t_en: Ah... General Huaiyan's gone. Wait, why does something feel off about what we talked about!?

t_ch: 金人巷中，飞霄等来了她多年未见的战友。
t_en: In Aurum Alley, Feixiao meets with her old comrade-in-arms whom she hasn't seen for many years...

t_ch: 能让大名鼎鼎的天击将军等我这么久，可算是赚足了面子。好久没见了，飞霄将军。
t_en: The illustrious Merlin's Claw waiting for me, and for so long, too? It's quite an honor. It's been a while, General Feixiao.

t_ch: 驭空姐姐，自上次分别，咱们快有三十年没见了吧？
t_en: It's been thirty years since we last saw each other, right, Yukong?

t_ch: 是啊，三十年前，你是曜青「青丘军」的先锋，我是罗浮「垂虹卫」的飞行士。没想到，再次见面你已成了将军，而我已经放弃了飞翔…如此想来，真是恍如隔世啊。
t_en: Yes, back then, you were the vanguard at the Yaoqing's Verdant Knights, and I was a pilot of the Luofu's Rainbowfall Fleet. Who would have thought that upon meeting again, you'd be a general, and I'd have given up flying... Really does feel like a lifetime ago.

t_ch: 其实也不能说三十年没见，毕竟「黄钟」共鸣系统里天天播放着你大捷的战报，我也算是日日得见你所向披靡的英姿。…你的身体还好吗？
t_en: Well, I wouldn't say I haven't seen you in thirty years. After all, your great victories are announced through the Yellowbell Resonance System every day, so I'm well aware of your great feats... How's your health holding up?

t_ch: 状况还算稳定。不知你还记不记得，当年在战阵中救起我的那位军医？
t_en: Still stable, I suppose. Do you still remember the medic who saved me in battle?

t_ch: 那个名字古怪、脾气也很古怪的医士…叫什么来着？我只记得他名字里沾个葱姜蒜什么的…
t_en: That healer with the odd name and peculiar temperament... What was his name again? Was it Pichu? Or Kaqiu?...

t_ch: 椒丘。受曜青仙舟丹鼎司的派遣，他已是我的幕僚和随身医士。这些年他一直在想法子控制我的「病情」。这么多年过去了，我还活得好好的，他功不可没。
t_en: Jiaoqiu. He's been my retainer and personal healer, delegated by the Alchemy Commission from the Xianzhou Yaoqing. Over the years, he's dedicated himself to managing my "condition." It's thanks to him that I'm still in good health today.

t_ch: 以我的出身，能一路走到今天，我很满足了。
t_en: Given my background, I'm happy to have made it this far.

t_ch: 知道你一切平安，我就放心了。那么，这次你和怀炎老先生前来，想必是领了元帅的命令吧？
t_en: I'm relieved to know that you're safe and sound. Well then, since you and Elder Huaiyan are here, I imagine you must have received orders from the Marshal?

t_ch: 飞霄，看在朋友的面子上，能告诉我，联盟打算如何降罪于罗浮的将军吗？
t_en: As your friend, may I ask how the Alliance intends to punish the General of the Luofu?

t_ch: 建木重生，让那些惯于躲在后方的老家伙们感到了害怕…他们怕孽物再度卷土重来，像三十年前那般。
t_en: The Arbor's rebirth has frightened the elders who lurk behind the scenes... They fear the resurgence of abominations, much like what happened thirty years ago.

t_ch: 虽然罗浮呈上报告解释了来龙去脉。但烬灭军团的入侵是否真的存在，星核猎手以及星穹列车到底如何介入此事，其中却有众多细节缺失。
t_en: Although the report from the Luofu explained all the details, we don't know if the Ruin Legion really invaded, or how exactly the Stellaron Hunters and the Astral Express became involved. This puzzle has many missing pieces.

t_ch: 想必你也知道，消失多年的逃犯镜流再度出现了。这次她带来一个化外民和一具棺材，自称向元帅献上「与神相争的法子」。
t_en: As you know, the fugitive Jingliu, who mysteriously disappeared many years ago has resurfaced. This time, she has brought along an outworlder and a coffin, claiming to offer the Marshal "a method to fight against the Aeons."

t_ch: 罗浮的龙师也状告景元不顾盟谊，说他放任流徙在外的饮月龙尊重返罗浮，打开鳞渊境古海，干扰持明守望建木的责任。
t_en: The Luofu Preceptor has also leveled accusations against Jing Yuan for neglecting the Alliance's principles. She asserts that Jing Yuan enabled the exiled Imbibitor Lunae to reenter the Luofu, thereby unlocking the Lunarescent Depths within Scalegorge Waterscape, which in turn disrupted the Vidyadhara's dutiful watch over the Ambrosial Arbor.

t_ch: 这些都是我今天不得不踏入罗浮仙舟的原因。
t_en: It is for these reasons that I have come here to the Luofu today.

t_ch: 职责所在，我本来不必向外人解释这么多。但你我曾并肩作战，我不打算瞒你，也希望这些话你过耳就忘，可以吗？
t_en: Well, duty calls. Perhaps I shouldn't have mentioned all of this to an uninvolved person. But since we once fought together, I didn't want to keep you in the dark. Perhaps pretending you didn't hear any of this would be for the best.

t_ch: 我明白了，抱歉，是我失仪了。我也知道自己现在不该多嘴为景元将军辩护…但，你也了解我的性格。
t_en: I understand. I'm sorry, I was out of line. I know I shouldn't be defending General Jing Yuan right now... but, well, you know how I am.

t_ch: 罗浮自饮月之乱结束后，享有数百年的安定，这其中景元将军的擘画功不可没。
t_en: The Luofu has enjoyed centuries of stability since the end of the Sedition of Imbibitor Lunae, much of which can be attributed to General Jing Yuan's masterful strategizing.

t_ch: 可惜对长生种而言，只要活得够久，就总会迎来摧毁往日累积一切的失败——敌人等待的就是那一刻。
t_en: Unfortunately, for long-life species, enduring through the ages always culminates in a failure that undoes all previous achievements — a moment that our adversaries relish.

t_ch: 说的不错。所以这次我前来，还有另一个目的：探视「呼雷」。
t_en: That's true. And that's why I'm also here for another purpose — to visit Hoolay.

t_ch: 呼雷？你是说那个呼雷？那个步离人战首？从七百多年前就被拘禁在罗浮幽囚狱中的呼雷？
t_en: Hoolay? You mean that Hoolay? The borisin Warhead? The same Hoolay who has been imprisoned in the Shackling Prison for over seven centuries?

t_ch: 「狐人大敌」，「永世不赦」，「直至天地荒灭」之类的…我记不住那么多判词，就是那个家伙。
t_en: "The nemesis of the foxians, who will never be forgiven and shall be imprisoned until the end of the cosmos..." I can't quite remember the exact wording, but yes, the very same Hoolay.

t_ch: 但对呼雷的探视，从来是由曜青仙舟天舶司派出使节，百年一次。为何你要在这节骨眼上……
t_en: Usually only emissaries from the Xianzhou Yaoqing's Sky-Faring Commission visit him once every century. Why do you have to visit him now, of all times...?

t_ch: 狐人与仙舟盟誓共讨孽物，除了解放同胞，求的还有正义：那只狼牙恶兽应当永远被关在幽囚狱里，日日受罚。
t_en: The foxians and the Alliance made a pact to combat the abominations, aiming to achieve justice and free their kin. That werewolf monster is to be forever imprisoned in the dark recesses of The Shackling Prison, facing unending retribution.

t_ch: 以罗浮的状况，曜青内部颇为担心呼雷的关押情况。以往每隔百年一次的例行探视已经安不下老家伙们的心了。
t_en: Given the situation on the Luofu, those on the Yaoqing are concerned about Hoolay's imprisonment. I'm afraid that the routine visit every century is no longer sufficient to ease their concerns.

t_ch: 我被派来安他们的心。
t_en: That's why I was sent here — to reassure them.

t_ch: 尽是些坏消息。
t_en: It's all bad news.

t_ch: 也不全是。至少，许多事情未必有你想的这么糟糕。你托我寻找的目标，已有了眉目。
t_en: Well, not everything. There might be a silver lining. By the way, I've found some clues about the thing you asked for.

t_ch: 快讲讲！
t_en: Tell me more!

t_ch: 青丘军按照你提供的航路，找到了「鸣火」商会的舰船残骸。很遗憾，里面没有货物，也没有任何幸存者。
t_en: The Verdant Knights followed the route you mentioned and discovered the wreckage of the Whistling Flames ship. Unfortunately, there were no survivors, and no cargo.

t_ch: 不过，在我们找到失事地点之前，就已经有人到过现场了。
t_en: However, someone had already been on the scene before we arrived.

t_ch: 是我们的人吗？还是公司的人？
t_en: Our people? Or someone from the IPC?

t_ch: 都不是。驭空，你知道{RUBY_B#天才俱乐部#81}阮•梅{RUBY_E#}吗？
t_en: No, neither. Yukong, have you heard of a person named {RUBY_B#Genius Society #81}Ruan Mei{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 公司派驻仙舟的商业代表之一。\n论性格是个欺软怕硬的家伙，长久在公司工作的经验让他懂得至少表面上要<color=#dbc291ff>遵守规矩</color>。
t_en: One of the representatives stationed on Xianzhou by the IPC.\nHe picks on the weak but fears the powerful. His experience in the IPC has taught him that he has to <color=#dbc291ff>abide by the rules</color>, at least on the surface.

t_ch: 来自仙舟「曜青」的云骑将军。\n受元帅之命前来「观礼」演武仪典。不过她此行真正的目标是观察罗浮在建木灾异之后的情况。\n这位将军予人的第一印象直爽而凌厉。想在<color=#dbc291ff>言语上欺瞒她</color>，也许并不是什么好主意。
t_en: The Cloud Knight General from the Xianzhou Yaoqing.\nShe came to "observe" the Wardance under the orders of the Marshal. However, the true purpose of her trip is to observe the situation on the Luofu after the Ambrosial Arbor crisis.\nThe first impression of this general is that she is straightforward and stern. Therefore, trying to <color=#dbc291ff>deceive her</color> is probably not a good idea.

t_ch: 公司派驻仙舟的商业代表之一。他虚伪地维持着公司文化自傲，但也压抑着对公司的不满与抱怨。\n斯科特耀武扬威的基础都是公司的<color=#dbc291ff>权威与福利</color>，小心不要让他<color=#dbc291ff>趁机仗势欺人</color>。
t_en: One of the representatives stationed on Xianzhou by the IPC. While he hypocritically remains proud of the IPC's corporate culture, he has also suppressed his dissatisfaction and resentment for the Corporation.\nThe basis of Skott's arrogance is the <color=#dbc291ff>authority and benefits</color> afforded by the IPC. Don't let him <color=#dbc291ff>take advantage of the situation and bully others</color>.

t_ch: 一日奔波，你们来到浥尘客栈安顿歇息……
t_en: After a day of running around, you finally arrive at Petrichor Inn for a well-deserved rest...

t_ch: 第二天
t_en: Day two.

t_ch: 这一夜，你被面见将军的不安，以及演武仪典召开前的热闹人流搅扰得难以成眠。
t_en: This night, your restlessness from meeting the general and the bustling crowd before the Wardance keeps you from falling asleep.

t_ch: 睡得迷迷糊糊……
t_en: I'm still feeling sleepy...

t_ch: 奇怪，是我起晚了吗？三月和丹恒这是去了哪儿？
t_en: Hmm, did I oversleep? Where did March and Dan Heng go?

t_ch: （三月七啊三月七，才刚学剑。就三心二意想要翘课了吗？）
t_en: (Oh come on, March 7th! Really now? You just got into swordplay training. Are you sick of it already? Is that why you want to skip practice?)

t_ch: （不行，可不能教两位老师生气…）
t_en: (No, both my masters will be mad if I do that...)

t_ch: 继续「检查」吧…
t_en: Keep looking...

t_ch: 留神四周。
t_en: Pay attention to your surroundings.

t_ch: 需要准备的东西不少…
t_en: There's quite a number of things to prepare...

t_ch: 「船」最重要。
t_en: The ship is paramount.

t_ch: 优先完成任务…
t_en: Complete the mission first...

t_ch: 务必完成末度大人的要求
t_en: Mok Tok's request must be seen to.

t_ch: 明白。
t_en: Understood.

t_ch: 奇怪…？
t_en: Strange...?

t_ch: 这附近似乎有什么味道？
t_en: What's this smell around here?

t_ch: 任务要紧。
t_en: This mission is critical.

t_ch: 你注意到鳞渊境的幽囚狱建造在古海之下，是不断向下延展的柱状结构。冥差们在这块屏幕前管理着不同囚室、不同囚犯的实时信息。
t_en: You notice the Scalegorge Waterscape's Shackling Prison was built at the bottom of the ancient sea, as a column structure that extends endlessly into the depths. Spiritfarers monitor and manage real-time information of prison cells and prisoners on this screen.

t_ch: 此处的玉兆散发着奇异的光辉，不知演算着何物。也许是囚室的状态，也许是各处的安全状况……
t_en: The jade abaci here glow with an unusual luminescence as they perform their calculations, possibly on the status of the prison cells or the security of each area.

t_ch: 向下望去，无数监狱囚室如同蜂巢般密密麻麻展开，没入视线不可及的黑暗。
t_en: Looking down, you see a honeycomb of densely-packed prison cells extending deep into the darkness beyond your view.

t_ch: 你暗暗猜测这里究竟能关押多少犯人？三千？三万？三十万……？也许这里只是幽囚狱系统小小的一角。
t_en: You hazard a guess on the number of prisoners they are capable of holding here... Three thousand? Thirty thousand? Three hundred thousand? Wait, this may not even be the entire Shackling Prison!

t_ch: 寒鸦拦住了你的去路。她身负要务，必须确保你一路所至之处由她决定。
t_en: Hanya blocks your way forward. She has been entrusted with the important task of ensuring that you only explore areas she deems safe.

t_ch: 巨大的威灵丑面凶恶地瞪视着你，仿佛在警告你行止戒慎。
t_en: Towering spirits with gruesome appearances glare menacingly at you, as if warning you to proceed with caution.

t_ch: 它们的目光交汇处，有一个黑黢黢的空洞，不知作何之用？
t_en: Their gazes converge at a pitch-black void, and you wonder what it's for.

t_ch: 形如撑天巨柱的「钥匙」贯入「锁孔」中，开启通往镇伏凶犯的幽狱之底。
t_en: When a "key" in the shape of a colossal sky-bearing column slots into the "keyhole," it unlocks the path to the Shackling Prison crypts, where criminals of high severity are held in subdued captivity.

t_ch: 这便是「断狱轮钥」，负责把守重刑犯的最终门关。
t_en: That was the Nether Key — the key that keeps felons locked away behind the ultimate gate.

t_ch: 一只机巧鸟，双眼幽光闪烁，它的脑袋不时颤动，看向空无一物的地方。
t_en: You see a cycrane twitching its head every so often, staring into space with a ghostly glint in its eyes.

t_ch: 它能看到什么肉眼不能见之物吗？你忍不住打了个寒颤。
t_en: Is there something that cannot be seen with the naked eye? You can't help but shudder at that thought.

t_ch: 你看向貊泽，他微微摇头，示意你现在实在不是个插科打诨的好时机。
t_en: You look at Moze, who shakes his head lightly at you. Guess this isn't really the time for jokes.

t_ch: 不知是否是幽囚狱的气氛过于沉肃压抑，教他连开口说话的兴致也欠奉。
t_en: Perhaps the stifling and somber mood of the Shackling Prison has gotten to him, that's why he's neither up for chatting nor humoring you.

t_ch: 你听从雪衣的吩咐靠近那只「小鸟」。当你向它伸出手时，它仿佛感应到了你的存在，将视线投向你。
t_en: You follow Xueyi's instructions and approach the bird. When you extend your hand in its direction, it turns to look at you, seemingly sensing your presence.

t_ch: 人类无法听见的唿哨声让这只幽狱的信使醒了过来。
t_en: A whistle inaudible to human ears awakens this particular messenger of the Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 唤醒「信使」。
t_en: Awaken the Messenger.

t_ch: 造访「幽狱底层」，授权完成。
t_en: Visiting the Shackling Prison's crypts. Authorization complete.

t_ch: 巨大的金属残骸横陈在地，但不知为何你很清楚它并未完全死去。它的外壳下仿生组织正在不断修复。
t_en: Large pieces of metal scraps are strewn all over the floor. You can't put your finger on it, but you're certain that this thing is still alive. Bionic tissues are relentlessly regenerating beneath their exoskeleton.

t_ch: 肢体扭曲的步离遗骸，这头巨狼肢体似曾被牢牢捆锁。
t_en: These are the remains of a borisin. Judging from their twisted limbs, it is likely that this massive wolf once had their limbs restrained.

t_ch: 这名步离人似乎尚未来得及反应便被人从后方刺穿。你不清楚这究竟是曜青使者做的，还是判官的手笔。
t_en: Whoever impaled this borisin from behind probably caught them off guard. It could be a judge or a Yaoqing messenger.

t_ch: 力战倒下的武弁。她的武器不翼而飞，也许遗落在了监狱某处。
t_en: This warden fought to her death, and her weapon's nowhere to be seen. She must have lost it somewhere in the prison.

t_ch: 显然这名武弁在殉职前经历了一场激烈的战斗。你将她的面具重新盖回她的脸上，祈祷她的英灵能得享安息。
t_en: You can clearly tell that the warden was serving her duty in an intense battle when she died. You reset the mask over her face and pray for her heroic soul to rest in peace.

t_ch: 你察觉到这个身着云骑甲胄的死者似乎并非普通的狐人，他的身躯似乎经历过剧烈的变形——来不及缩回的鬣毛布满了脖颈。
t_en: The corpse in a Cloud Knight's armor catches your attention. They do not seem to be your average foxian, for one. This body looks like it underwent a drastic shapeshifting, one that failed to conceal the guard hairs around their neck.

t_ch: 死者前突的下颚利齿横生，与那些死去的步离人颇为神似。
t_en: Sharp fangs grew unchecked along the deceased's protruded lower jaw, bearing an uncanny resemblance to the other borisin corpses.

t_ch: 你渐渐明白步离人为何能不受阻碍地闯入幽狱底部。他们不仅伪装成了云骑，更伪装成了狐人的模样。
t_en: You begin to see why the borisin could easily infiltrate the lower levels of the Shackling Prison. They not only shapeshifted into the Cloud Knights, but into foxians too.

t_ch: 机巧鸟扑腾起来，它颤颤巍巍地飞离幽囚狱底部，一头扎进幽深的通道深处。
t_en: The cycrane flaps its wings and unsteadily flies out of the lower levels of the Shackling Prison, charging straight into the depths of the shadowy passage.

t_ch: 悬梯的机关似乎被捷足先登的劫狱者们早早破坏，无论如何摆弄都毫无动静。
t_en: None of the assembly methods or formations work. The hanging ladder's mechanism must have been destroyed by those prison raiders that got here first.

t_ch: 眼下你们只能靠自己的双腿追上那些犯人。
t_en: You'll have to run on your own two feet to catch up to those prisoners now.

t_ch: 这些入侵者拥有非凡的力量，他们无需持配武器，他们自己就是最危险的武器。
t_en: These intruders possess extraordinary strength. They wield no weapons, for they are the deadliest weapons themselves.

t_ch: 步离人巨大的身形充满压迫感。即便死去多时，这堆肉山仍仿佛能随时跃起扑杀猎物一般，令人颤栗。
t_en: The borisin's large stature easily lends them an intimidating quality that stays with them even after their demise. Looking at this pile of borisin corpses would send chills down anyone's spine, for it feels as though they can reanimate and pounce onto their prey any moment.

t_ch: 尽管幽囚狱的狱卒饱经训练，但在步离人恐怖的冲击之下，仍然死伤惨重。
t_en: The prison guards at the Shackling Prison may be well-trained, but they likewise suffered deaths and casualties in the face of the borisin's horrifying assault.

t_ch: 武弁们发誓为把守幽狱奉献生命。但目睹她们的牺牲，你仍希望她们能安然活着。
t_en: Although the wardens had taken an oath to defend the Shackling Prison to the death, you cannot help but wish they could treasure their lives after witnessing their sacrifice.

t_ch: 这具金人勾魂使似乎在一击之间被敌人击碎。如此惊人的力量，也许是呼雷的手臂。
t_en: This aurumaton spectral envoy appears to have been smashed to smithereens with only a single strike. Hoolay likely slammed his arm in to deliver this shockingly destructive force.

t_ch: 勾魂使金属的头颅中似乎仍然闪动着一丝火花，它在击毙数倍于己的敌人后终于丧失了还手之力，惨遭拆解。
t_en: Sparks continue to fizzle atop the spectral envoy's metal head. Despite having killed enemies that outnumber them, they were eventually left without the means to fight back before being wrecked to pieces.

t_ch: 已接受使命「阻击逃犯」…
t_en: Mission accepted. Stop the fugitives...

t_ch: 吾等必恪尽职守…
t_en: We must fulfill our duty...

t_ch: 不可懈怠，继续追索
t_en: We mustn't tarry. Onward with the pursuit.

t_ch: 大人，犯人向那边逃去…
t_en: The fugitives fled that way...

t_ch: 我们来维持此处秩序…
t_en: Let's maintain the order here...

t_ch: 请一定要阻拦逃犯…
t_en: Please stop those fugitives...

t_ch: 经历短暂却致命的失联，被封锁幽囚狱重回掌控。尽管列车无名客一行并无大碍，但曜青来使中却有一人遭到劫持。
t_en: After a brief but devastating loss of communications, the locked-down Shackling Prison is once more under control. The Express' Nameless may have emerged unharmed, but one of the Yaoqing envoys has been taken hostage.

t_ch: 与此同时…长久镇于幽狱之底的步离人战首呼雷，已失去了踪迹。
t_en: Meanwhile, the borisin Warhead Hoolay, long confined to the depth of Shackling Prison, has vanished without a trace.

t_ch: 猎人与猎物，将在罗浮仙舟上展开追逐。
t_en: A pursuit between the hunter and the hunted will soon play out on the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 巨大的凶面漠然毫无响应，大门纹丝不动。
t_en: The intimidating countenance has no response whatsoever. The gate doesn't budge.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来了啊。
t_en: There you are, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 彦卿怎么也在这儿？
t_en: Why are you here too, Yanqing?

t_ch: 你该不会忘了昨天我们应承怀炎将军的事情吧？彦卿不敢怠慢，我特地起了个大早，来教授三月小姐剑术。
t_en: You haven't forgotten about our promise to General Huaiyan, have you? I woke up especially early, so I could start teaching Miss March swordplay.

t_ch: 哪有师父上门接徒弟的？
t_en: What kind of master waits on their apprentice's doorstep?

t_ch: 毕竟是应承了怀炎将军的要求，彦卿不敢怠慢。我特地起了个大早，来教授三月小姐剑术。
t_en: Well, it was a promise to General Huaiyan, after all. I woke up especially early, so I could start teaching Miss March swordplay.

t_ch: 三月，还不快拜见师父？
t_en: March? Show some respect to your master.

t_ch: 彦卿师父在上~
t_en: It's an honor to learn from you, Master Yanqing!

t_ch: 看幻戏和小说里，仙舟拜师学艺可得送上师父大礼，跪拜叩首。糟糕，我可什么都没准备。
t_en: In all those immersias and novels I watched, when apprentices want to pay their respects to their masters, they usually bring fancy gifts, kneel, and bow. I'm really sorry, but I haven't prepared anything yet.

t_ch: 哎，彦卿师父，你怎么脸红啦？
t_en: Hey, why are you blushing, Master Yanqing?

t_ch: 第一次听到别人叫自己师父，彦卿得稍微…适应一下……
t_en: This is the first time someone has called me master... I need to... get used to it...

t_ch: 不过，有言在先，习剑是为了锻炼体魄、心智与力量，不是朝夕可达的游戏。
t_en: Let me make it clear: Swordplay training is about improving your body, mind, and strength. It's not a casual game you can master overnight.

t_ch: 我答应了怀炎将军，要让三月小姐学会云骑剑术，登上演武仪典，以及至少击败一位对手。我一定会竭尽所能施教，但要是你有违师长教训……
t_en: I promised General Huaiyan that I'd teach you Cloud Knight swordplay, so you can participate in the Wardance and defeat at least one opponent. I'll do my best, but if you break your master's rules...

t_ch: 你、你要怎么样？
t_en: Wh—what will you do?

t_ch: 现在后悔还来得及吗？都怪我一时冲动。
t_en: Is it too late for regrets? I'm already regretting my impulsive decision.

t_ch: …说实话，我也不知该怎么办。毕竟我是第一天教人。那就拜托三月小姐规规矩矩，不许叫苦，也不准逃课。
t_en: ...Honestly, I'm not sure what I'll do either, because I've never been a teacher before. So, Miss March, please behave and don't skip classes or complain.

t_ch: 彦卿师父，你得支棱起来，狠狠培养她。
t_en: You need to toughen up and train her properly, Master Yanqing. Don't go easy on her.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你好狠的心啊。
t_en: You're so heartless, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 三月很乖的！
t_en: March is always a good girl!

t_ch: 本姑娘一言既出，星槎难追！既然答应了好好学，就从今天开始！
t_en: Fine. A promise is a promise. Since I promised to study hard, I'll do my best starting today!

t_ch: 好啊，要的就是这股精气神。
t_en: Great! That's the spirit.

t_ch: 彦卿，三月就拜托你了，可别对她太客气了。
t_en: March is in your hands now, Yanqing. Don't be too easy on her.

t_ch: 丹恒，你的心真是比你平日里的笑话还冷。
t_en: Dan Heng, do you even have a heart? Did you lose it somewhere?

t_ch: 说起来，云璃呢？
t_en: By the way, where's Yunli?

t_ch: 彦卿在司辰宫后花园处找了个僻静的地方，我们会在那儿一起为你上第一堂课。
t_en: I've found a quiet spot in the back garden of the Palace of Astrum for our first lesson together.

t_ch: 三月七朝你和丹恒挥了挥手，开始了她习剑之路的第一课……
t_en: March 7th waves to you and Dan Heng, embarking on the first lesson of her sword training journey...

t_ch: 第一天上课，就带着徒弟一起迟到，罗浮上的规矩怎么如此散漫？唉…真让为师寒心。
t_en: Seriously? It's the first day, and you two are already late? Why is everyone on the Luofu so laid-back? So disappointing.

t_ch: 啊，云璃师父已经到了。久等了！
t_en: Ah, Master Yunli you're already here. Sorry for keeping you waiting!

t_ch: 你该不会想一个人偷偷教她吧？太卑鄙啦！
t_en: Wait, were you trying to teach her in secret? That's sneaky!

t_ch: 让你见识见识，这就是罗浮人授徒传艺的周到礼数！哪怕是弟子，我也会将她亲自接来。
t_en: I'm just showing you what Luofu etiquette is all about. She might be my apprentice, but it's customary for the master to personally escort their apprentice to the place of learning.

t_ch: 作为东道主，我会传授三月小姐罗浮剑术的精华，然后让她在擂台上大获全胜。到时候，你就没法再对罗浮的剑法优劣评头论足了吧！
t_en: As the host, I'll be teaching Miss March the essence of Luofu swordplay, after which she'll emerge victorious in the Wardance ring. You won't be complaining about Luofu swordplay then!

t_ch: 一边待着去。我让你瞧瞧，朱明仙舟的剑士是如何宠爱弟子的。
t_en: Stand aside, rookie. Let me show you how we Zhuming swordmasters treat their apprentices.

t_ch: 三月七，快来啊，这是为师送给你的「反向拜师礼」，望你善加利用。
t_en: Quickly, over here, Miss March. This is a "reverse mentorship gift" from me to you. I hope you put it to good use.

t_ch: 这是…仙舟服饰？好漂亮呀！
t_en: What's this... Xianzhou clothing? It's so beautiful!

t_ch: 练剑需要身法步法配合，这套衣服剪裁贴身，便于活动。我还特意备了些小配饰，可是花了我不少心思。
t_en: Sword practice requires precise movements. This outfit is tailored to fit perfectly and allows for smooth movements. I even added some small accessories. I put a lot of thought into it.

t_ch: 云璃师父对我真好！
t_en: You're awesome, Master Yunli!

t_ch: 瞧见了吗，你拿什么和我比？我会让三月七学到朱明剑术的要义，让她以我的剑胜出擂台，哼哼。
t_en: See? See? How can you compete with me? I'll teach March 7th the essence of Zhuming swordplay, so she can win the contest with my sword skills.

t_ch: 我也不是没有准备。
t_en: Actually, I've prepared something too.

t_ch: 诶？彦卿师父也有礼物要送给我吗？
t_en: Huh? You have a gift for me too, Master Yanqing?

t_ch: 既然三月小姐来学剑，那总要有柄趁手的兵刃才行，就连夜置办了一套对剑。
t_en: Since you want to learn swordplay, Miss March, you'll need suitable weapons. So, I went out of my way to prepare a pair of swords overnight.

t_ch: 可惜时间仓促，来不及请匠人定制。我只好尽力选了模样好看，配重又适合初学者的武器。若是三月小姐不嫌弃，那就太好了。
t_en: Unfortunately, I didn't have time to ask the craftsman to customize the swords for you, but I did my best to choose ones that look nice and are suitable for a beginner. I hope you like them, Miss March.

t_ch: 谢谢彦卿师父！
t_en: Thank you, Master Yanqing!

t_ch: 没想到吧云璃？
t_en: Bet you didn't see that coming, did you, Yunli?

t_ch: 哼，真正的比试现在才刚刚开始。
t_en: Hmph, the real competition is just getting started.

t_ch: 这师拜得可真值啊！但我怎么觉得，事情开始有些往奇怪的苗头上发展了。
t_en: I'm so lucky to have two great masters! But why does it feel like things are getting a bit weird?

t_ch: 片刻后
t_en: Moments later

t_ch: 怎么样，师父们？这装扮还算合适吗？
t_en: So, what do you think, masters? Does this outfit suit me?

t_ch: 嗯嗯！不愧是我精挑细选的衣服，最适合你我这般的美少女剑士！
t_en: Perfect! I chose it carefully — it's perfect for beautiful young swordswomen like you and me!

t_ch: 闲话少叙，咱们开始上课吧。旁边这位是我请来的云骑教习，这第一堂课先和他过两招吧。
t_en: Alright, let's get started with the training. The person next to me is a Cloud Knight instructor I've brought in. For your first lesson, try exchanging a couple of moves with him.

t_ch: 哎？怎么这第一堂课就是实战训练？！会不会太激进了些？
t_en: Wait, we're having actual combat training for the first lesson!? Isn't that a bit too intense?

t_ch: 听说你练过射艺，有受训武事的底子，所以第一课当然要摸摸你的根基扎实不扎实。
t_en: Well, I heard you have some experience with archery and martial arts. The first thing we're going to do is see just how strong your fundamentals are.

t_ch: 来吧，上前一步，用你能想象到的最顺手的方式挥剑攻击！只有了解了你的肢体习惯，我们才知道该从哪儿开始，教你些什么。
t_en: Come on, step forward and strike with the sword in the most natural way you can think of! It's important for us to grasp your natural movements, so we can decide where to start and what you need to learn.

t_ch: 如果您准备好了，就请与在下对阵！
t_en: If you're ready, let's begin!

t_ch: 啊…哈哈…好的……
t_en: Ah... haha... okay...

t_ch: 准备好了吗？
t_en: Are you ready?

t_ch: 我准备好了…
t_en: I'm ready...

t_ch: 我准备好了…但你可得手下留情啊！
t_en: Yeah, I'm ready... but please go easy on me!

t_ch: 那就多有得罪了小姐！
t_en: Then I apologize in advance, Miss March!

t_ch: 我还要再准备一下…
t_en: Not really...

t_ch: 容我稍微再准备一下……
t_en: I need some more time to prepare myself...

t_ch: 不着急！慢慢准备！
t_en: No rush! Take your time!

t_ch: 请师父用茶！
t_en: Please have some tea, masters!

t_ch: 三月小姐瞧好了！
t_en: Pay close attention, Miss March.

t_ch: 罗浮剑术讲究剑走轻灵。
t_en: Luofu swordplay is all about being swift and agile in your movements.

t_ch: 别听彦卿的，大力出奇迹！
t_en: Ignore him. Strength is everything!

t_ch: 是这样吗？
t_en: Like this?

t_ch: 像模像样啊！
t_en: Well done!

t_ch: 心诚意正，来啦！
t_en: With a true heart and focused mind — here I come!

t_ch: 左青龙，右白虎，少吃碳水化合物！
t_en: Azure Dragon, White Tiger, eating less carbs!

t_ch: 你念叨什么呢？
t_en: What are you mumbling about?

t_ch: 这不是…剑诀嘛！
t_en: Isn't this... a sword technique? Shouldn't I be saying something cool?

t_ch: 有点感觉了。
t_en: I'm starting to get the hang of it.

t_ch: 顺手起来啦！瞧好了这招！
t_en: Alright, check out this move!

t_ch: 没想到第一堂课的强度就这么高……
t_en: I didn't expect the first lesson to be so intense...

t_ch: 三月小姐的身手灵活，腰肢柔软，真是练习罗浮剑术的绝好苗子。
t_en: Miss March has quick hands and a flexible body. She's a perfect fit for practicing Luofu swordplay.

t_ch: 不过正手不精，反手乏力，挥剑时各种动作太过潦草…这也是初学者的通病了。无妨，只要习练朱明剑法，一切就会好起来的。
t_en: However, she lacks strength, and her strikes were a bit unfocused... but don't worry, that's totally normal for beginners. Once she starts practicing Zhuming swordplay, she'll make heaps of progress.

t_ch: 我决定了，下一课，三月小姐从练习——
t_en: Given the situation, I believe Miss March should start by working on —

t_ch: 步法开始。发力开始。
t_en: Her footwork. Her strength.

t_ch: 你到底懂不懂剑术啊？
t_en: Seriously, do you actually know anything about swordplay or what?

t_ch: 我还想问你呢！双剑本就强调灵活应敌、左右相援，还有什么比步法更重要吗？
t_en: I could ask you the same thing! Dual swords require agility, so what's more important than footwork?

t_ch: 战阵强弱并不总靠长处，还要看短处。双剑的短处恰恰在于发力。双臂乏力，步法灵活又有什么用？这又不是跳舞。
t_en: Instead of focusing on her strengths, we should address her weaknesses. The drawback of wielding two swords is not generating enough force. What good is being quick on your feet if you don't have strength? It's not like we're dancing here.

t_ch: 但凡学有所成的剑士都懂得「扬长避短」。先发扬长处，再反哺短处，这才是正确的学习方式。
t_en: Skilled swordmasters know how to play to their strengths and work on their weaknesses. Start with what you're good at, then tackle your weaknesses. That's the right way to learn.

t_ch: 哼哼，小少爷真不愧是空想理论派啊！
t_en: You're quite the theorist, huh?

t_ch: 空想？你自诩能听懂剑说话，这又算什么？空想社交派？
t_en: Theorist? You claim to be able to talk to swords, so what does that make you? A lunatic?

t_ch: 唉唉，怎么才第一节课，两位师父就吵起来了！你们快别吵了，步法和发力我早晚都是要学的，先学哪个不都一样嘛？
t_en: Hey, it's only my first lesson, and you're already arguing! Come on, calm down, masters! I'll have to improve both my footwork and strength anyway, so it doesn't matter which one comes first!

t_ch: 不一样！！！三月七，你听我的准没错！
t_en: But it does matter!!! Just listen to me, March 7th!

t_ch: 三月七的剑术训练，就这么开始了。
t_en: Thus begins the journey of March 7th's sword training.

t_ch: 此后，又过了些日子……
t_en: Time marches on, and days turn to more...

t_ch: 步法开始。发力开始。
t_en: Her footwork. Her strength.

t_ch: 步法开始。发力开始。
t_en: Her footwork. Her strength.

t_ch: 姬子、杨叔、帕姆：见信如晤。我们在罗浮一切都好，请各位放心。你们的旅行还顺利吧？
t_en: Dear Himeko, Mr. Yang, and Pom-Pom, We're all good here on the Xianzhou Luofu, so no need to worry! By the way, how's your trip going?

t_ch: 最近这段日子，我因为某些机缘巧合，成了两位云骑剑士的弟子，跟随他们修习剑术。
t_en: As for me, I've somehow become the apprentice to two Cloud Knight swordmasters, and I've been honing my sword skills with their guidance.

t_ch: 两位师父十分严厉，使我深刻感受到了修习剑术之艰辛。
t_en: Both masters are super strict, giving me a real taste of how hard sword training can be.

t_ch: 我本想拉{NICKNAME}下水，{F#她}{M#他}不肯。我又想拉丹恒下水，彦卿师父不肯。
t_en: I tried to drag {NICKNAME} into this, but {F#she}{M#he} refused. Then I tried to rope Dan Heng in, but Master Yanqing wouldn't have it.

t_ch: 尽管如此，我却从未因艰辛而退缩。在短短几周的时间里，我的剑术突飞猛进。两位师父都夸我是世间少有的剑术奇才，争先恐后地用各自的方法教导我。
t_en: Still, I didn't let the difficulty get to me. In just a few weeks, my sword skills have improved a lot. Both my masters think I have unique talents in swordplay and are literally fighting each other to teach me their skills.

t_ch: 如今，在两位师父的悉心教导下，我的剑术已小有所成。等回到列车以后，我一定要给诸位露一小手，让你们对我刮目相看！
t_en: Thanks to their guidance, I've actually made some progress. When I get back to the Express, I'll definitely show off my skills and impress you all!

t_ch: 三月七，翘首期盼回信。
t_en: Looking forward to your reply. Yours, March 7th.

t_ch: 洞天昼夜流转，演武仪典召开的时间不知不觉悄然临近。
t_en: Days and nights fly by in the delve, as the Wardance fast approaches.

t_ch: 在两位师父的严厉督导下，三月七苦练不辍，几乎患上了网球肘。
t_en: Under the strict supervision of her two masters, March 7th trains tirelessly, almost developing tennis elbow.

t_ch: 那一日，练剑结束后……
t_en: On that day, after the sword training...

t_ch: 其中一位是之前咱们曾见过的彦卿小弟弟。另一位，则是朱明将军怀炎的徒孙女，云璃。
t_en: One of them is Yanqing, the boy we've all met before. The other is Yunli, the granddaughter of General Huaiyan from the Xianzhou Zhuming.

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 可是那几个小崽子…
t_en: But those little brats...

t_ch: 很好，今天就练到这儿吧。
t_en: Well done. Let's call it a day.

t_ch: 三月小姐的双剑技巧已经小有所成。即便是到了演武仪典之上与人争锋，也绝不逊色。
t_en: Miss March's sword skills are really coming along. She'll hold her own just fine in the Wardance.

t_ch: 莫非我有机会打败两位师父？
t_en: Maybe I have a chance of defeating my masters?

t_ch: 难道说…我有机会在演武台上打败两位师父？
t_en: Perhaps... I might have a shot at beating both masters in the ring?

t_ch: 想什么呢，你不过练了这么短的时间，怎么可能？
t_en: Get real, you've only been practicing for a short while. How could that possibly happen?

t_ch: 以三月小姐的天资，若再肯下个几十年的苦功，也不是没有可能击败云璃的。
t_en: With your talent, Miss March, if you dedicate a few more decades to training, you might eventually be able to defeat Yunli.

t_ch: 呸呸呸！
t_en: Bah!

t_ch: 几十年啊…那时候我可都变成老婆婆了…不成不成。
t_en: Decades...? But I'll be an old lady by then... No, no.

t_ch: 那到时候就看我拿下一胜！
t_en: Just watch, I'll win a fight when the time comes!

t_ch: 到时候就看我在演武赛场上大显身手，拿下一胜，告慰两位！
t_en: Just you wait and see! I'll show off my skills in the ring and win a match. I'll make both of you proud!

t_ch: 没想到短短几周的光景，三月七竟然练得有模有样。我算是体会到爷爷当年教我剑法时，总是露出欣慰笑容的心情了。
t_en: Can you believe it? March 7th has actually become a pretty decent swordmaster in such a short time. Now I understand why grandpa always had a grin on his face while training me.

t_ch: 那最好是欣慰的笑。
t_en: Are you sure he wasn't laughing at you?

t_ch: 一边去！
t_en: Shut up!

t_ch: 嘿嘿～说来说去还是得归功于两位师父教得好！
t_en: It's all thanks to your amazing guidance, masters!

t_ch: 双剑小有所成，三月小姐要是想更进一步，也可以试试其他的仙舟剑器，增添手感。
t_en: Miss March, you're really getting the hang of wielding dual swords. If you're keen on advancing, trying out different Xianzhou blades could improve your touch.

t_ch: 唔，我想想…仙舟的剑器里，哪一种最厉害啊？单剑、重剑、还是飞剑？
t_en: Hmm, let me see... Which sword is the most powerful? Single sword? Greatsword? Or maybe a flying sword?

t_ch: 没有最厉害的剑，只有更厉害的剑士。你彦卿师父喜欢使唤多把飞剑，但你云璃师父只用一把剑，在丹鼎司里不还是把他揍得吱哇乱叫？
t_en: There's no such thing as the "most powerful" sword. It's all about the skill of the swordmaster. Yanqing wields several flying swords, while I only wield one, but remember how I kicked his butt at the Alchemy Commission?

t_ch: 第一，我压根没有吱哇乱叫！第二，你压根不是我的对手！第三，要不要现在试试谁能把谁揍得吱哇乱叫？
t_en: First, you didn't kick my butt! Second, you'll never kick my butt! Third, how about we settle this right now and see who kicks whose butt?

t_ch: 好啊！要是赢了你，你就乖乖退出演武仪典的擂台赛，如何？
t_en: Yeah, I'm up for that! And if I kick your butt, you'll drop out of the Wardance. Deal?

t_ch: 师父们怎么又吵起来了！我明明感到这些天你们的气氛融洽多了……
t_en: Why are you two arguing again? I thought things had been improving between you lately...

t_ch: 听说罗浮与朱明两位将军的高徒原本预定登上演武擂台一较高下，却不知因为什么缘故突然联手教起了徒弟来。这流言竟是真的。
t_en: There was talk that the leading disciples of the Luofu and Zhuming generals were supposed to face off in the Wardance, but for some reason, they suddenly teamed up to train an apprentice of their own. Turns out the rumors are true.

t_ch: 明日便是演武仪典举行的日子了，两位不各自砥砺锋芒，怎么还在这儿醉心教学？
t_en: Tomorrow is the big day of the Wardance. Shouldn't you two be focusing on honing your skills instead of teaching swordplay here?

t_ch: 啊，你是…那个…呃，那个…对了！曜青来的粉毛狐狸！
t_en: Ah, you're... um, you're... That's right! You're the pink-haired fox from the Yaoqing!

t_ch: 这位是椒丘先生。
t_en: This is Mr. Jiaoqiu.

t_ch: 这位是曜青将军帐下的医士，椒丘先生。
t_en: This is Mr. Jiaoqiu, the healer working for the General of the Xianzhou Yaoqing.

t_ch: 噗…「粉毛狐狸」…
t_en: *chuckle* "Pink-haired fox..."

t_ch: 噗…
t_en: Hahaha...

t_ch: 你笑什么？你也是粉毛！
t_en: What are you laughing at? You've got pink-hair, too!

t_ch: 不才椒丘，是曜青将军帐下的医士。
t_en: I'm Jiaoqiu, the healer working for the General of the Xianzhou Yaoqing.

t_ch: 我明白了，你是曜青派来参加演武仪典的选手，所以来这儿偷师？
t_en: Ah, got it. So, you're the participant attending the Wardance on behalf of the Yaoqing, and you were trying to sneak a peek at our training?

t_ch: 言重了，椒丘对武事一窍不通，只是被将军派来办些公务手续，无意打扰了两位的教学。见谅，我这就走。
t_en: Sorry for the misunderstanding. I don't know anything about martial arts. I'm just here on the general's orders to take care of some official business. I didn't mean to interrupt your training. I'll be on my way.

t_ch: 既然不懂剑术，那你刚才又在一旁笑个什么劲？
t_en: If you know nothing about martial arts, why were you smirking earlier?

t_ch: 鄙人只是对三月小姐「该学什么」的问题心有戚戚，忍不住凑了过来。
t_en: Well, my curiosity got the better of me I suppose. When I heard Miss March's pondering about what to learn, I couldn't help but wander over.

t_ch: 以鄙人的职业经验来看，剁刀、片刀、切刀、雕刀虽然同属刀具，但就像烹饪中的煎、炒、煮、炸一般，只是供人施展的技巧。如何使用，要考虑食材本身的特性。
t_en: From my professional experience, cleavers, slicers, chopping knives, and carving knives are all just tools, kind of like frying, sautéing, boiling, and deep-frying in cooking. They're just ways for people to show off their skills. How you use them really depends on the ingredients you're working with.

t_ch: 好比两位的剑法教学，若是顺着食材——我是说弟子的天性，以更适合的烹饪手法——我是说传授更适合她天性的技巧，才能令她事半功倍！
t_en: It's like your sword teaching methods. If you align your "ingredient" — in other words your apprentice's natural tendencies with the right "cooking method" — by which I mean the teaching method that best suits her, she'll make double the progress in half the time!

t_ch: 好比紫金茄要油炸、赤云椒要爆炒、黄石牛肉要焖煮。发掘食材——我是说弟子的天性，就是咱们的工作。
t_en: For example, Golden Eggplant tastes best when fried, Cloud Peppers when stir-fried, and Yellow Boulder Beef when simmered. It's all about discovering the nature of the ingredients... uh, I mean, apprentice.

t_ch: 都给我说饿了。
t_en: I'm getting hungry.

t_ch: 都给我说饿了。
t_en: I'm getting hungry.

t_ch: 都给我说饿了。
t_en: I'm getting hungry.

t_ch: 这报菜名，都把我给说饿了。
t_en: All this talk about food is making me hungry.

t_ch: 你不是医士嘛，怎么谈起做菜来了。
t_en: Aren't you a healer? Why are you talking about food?

t_ch: 是比喻，我加了点比喻。鄙人所师从的医方派别名曰「染指派」，是曜青仙舟上独有的医术，偏爱以食疗济愈病患。所以做菜的事情，我也略懂一二。
t_en: Well, it's just a metaphor. The medicinal school I follow on the Xianzhou Yaoqing is called the Ranzhi School, that specializes in food therapy, so it's only natural that I know a thing or two about cooking.

t_ch: 所以说…你是将军的厨子？
t_en: So... you're the general's cook?

t_ch: 咳，是医士！不想当医士的厨子算不上好的将军幕僚。算了，你就当我是个厨子吧。
t_en: I'm a healer! But anyway, a cook who isn't interested in health doesn't make for a good advisor. Fine, call me a cook if you want.

t_ch: 看你们的眼神，显然是误会我椒丘只是个妄议武学的孱弱文人。其实，我也不是对杀人技一窍不通的哦～毕竟「医道」本就是生杀一体之术。
t_en: Seeing the way you're looking at me, it's obvious you think I'm just some feeble academic who likes to blabber on about martial arts. But in reality, I know a thing or two about killing. After all, the art of healing inherently encompasses both life and death.

t_ch: 怎么突然跟小孩较起劲来了？
t_en: Why suddenly getting all serious with kids?

t_ch: 这人怎么跟小孩子较起劲来了。
t_en: Why is this guy suddenly getting all serious with kids?

t_ch: 看得出来，他真的是急了…
t_en: Seems like he just got offended...

t_ch: 成年人想从孩子手里找回场子…唉。
t_en: Seriously, trying to save face in front of kids? *sigh*

t_ch: 我手中这瓶药，你们可识得？
t_en: Do you recognize this bottle of medicine in my hand?

t_ch: 不认识。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 这叫「颠踬散」！是用域外奇花「押不芦」提炼浓缩而成的汤剂。
t_en: This is called "Tumbledust"! An extract from an exotic flower named "Yabruh."

t_ch: 毒药？
t_en: Is it poison?

t_ch: 哎，是毒药还是救命良药，端看医者用心如何。为病人做伐骨洗髓、开膛破腹的手术前，只消一滴，便能让人不知疼痛。但……
t_en: Well, it depends on how it's used. With just one drop, it's able to numb a patient's body during surgery, making them painless throughout the entire process. However...

t_ch: 若是剂量再多些，浓度再高些，便会放慢代谢，教人血流不凝，乃至五感尽失——虽是老病不侵的长生种服下了也不能免。
t_en: Increase the dose or the potency, and it'll slow the metabolism, making the blood thin and resulting in the loss of all senses. Even long-life species cannot escape its effects.

t_ch: 这东西可以救人，也可以杀人，派上的用场可比你们手中的刀剑多多了。
t_en: This thing can save lives or take them. It's more powerful than the swords in your hands.

t_ch: 彦卿还是更愿意将胜负放在剑锋之上，而不是…呃……
t_en: That may be so, but still I prefer settling things with a sword than... you know...

t_ch: 确实误会你了。你不是孱弱文人，你是无耻文人。
t_en: Looks like I did get you all wrong. You're not a feeble scholar, but a sinister and despicable one.

t_ch: 欸欸欸，怎么突然骂起人来了？我也不过是给大家普及医药知识，可不是要教唆各位投毒啊。
t_en: Hey, hey, why the insults all of a sudden? I'm just sharing some medical knowledge here, not persuading you to poison anyone.

t_ch: 椒丘先生一谈起毒药就满脸兴奋，也不知道算是正大光明还是阴险卑鄙……
t_en: Seems like you get real excited when talking about poison. I can't tell if that's an honorable thing or sinister...

t_ch: 假设现在有两个人，一个阴险卑鄙地站着，另一个光明正大地躺着。你们倒是说说看，那个躺着的有什么办法去控诉那个站着的「阴险卑鄙」呢？
t_en: Picture this: Two individuals, the one standing is full of malice, the other lying down is honorable and righteous. How can the one who's lying down label the one standing as "sinister?"

t_ch: 战阵之上，死生刹那，万念成空。「活下去」便是唯一的道理。
t_en: In the throes of combat, where life and death hinge on a singular moment, every idea fades into nothingness. The only thing that matters is "staying alive."

t_ch: 但凡能从战阵中活着回来，一切价值都会被重新定义。光明磊落也好，阴险卑鄙也罢，在我看来，都轻如鸿毛。
t_en: Surviving the battlefield reshapes all notions of worth. Be it integrity or treachery, in my eyes, their significance is negligible.

t_ch: 椒丘先生小看了我和云璃，我和她年纪虽小，也是上过战场的。
t_en: Perhaps you've underestimated Yunli and me, Mr. Jiaoqiu. We may be young, but we've seen our fair share of war.

t_ch: 失敬失敬。既然如此，你们也该知道演武仪典不过是争个赛场热闹，为何如此上心。
t_en: Well, well, then you should know that the Wardance is nothing more than a contest. So, why are you so focused on it?

t_ch: 被选为演武仪典的守擂者时，我也曾问过将军，云骑上阵杀敌是本分，为何还要在擂台上挥剑取悦观众？
t_en: When I was appointed as the ringmaster for the Wardance, I asked the general: "We Cloud Knights are supposed to charge into the fray and slay enemies. Why do we have to swing swords in a ring just to please an audience?"

t_ch: 将军回答我，「入阵出剑，登擂示剑；以一剑出鞘，敛百剑锋芒」。
t_en: And this is how the general replied: "To unsheathe your sword in a ring is no different than on the battlefield, as your sword reveals the might of all Cloud Knights."

t_ch: 演武仪典是个彰显武德，结交四方盟友的好机会。悬剑于仪典之上，出鞘而不伤，展示的不仅是剑，也是云骑的武德威仪。
t_en: The Wardance is the perfect chance to showcase martial virtue and forge alliances from all over the cosmos. When we unsheathe the sword without drawing blood, we not only display our might but also the martial virtue of the Cloud Knights.

t_ch: 这话说的倒是颇有见地，是鄙人见识短浅了。那么彦卿兄弟，我抵达罗浮许久，还无缘见识这次演武仪典的举办场地。
t_en: That's quite an insightful statement, Yanqing. Well, my apologies for being so short-sighted. I've been on the Luofu for quite some time, but I haven't had the chance to see the ceremony venue for myself yet.

t_ch: 如今听你侃侃而谈，心中倒是升起了几分好奇，不知你能否带我过去瞧瞧？
t_en: Hearing you speak so highly of it has piqued my curiosity. Would you mind showing me around?

t_ch: 椒丘先生想去观赏「竞锋舰」？好啊！云璃和三月小姐也一定没见过。这样吧，我带各位去见识见识。
t_en: You want to see the Skysplitter ship where the Wardance will be held? Let's go! I bet Yunli and Miss March haven't seen it either, right? Well then, I'll give you all a tour.

t_ch: 立刻出发！
t_en: Let's go now!

t_ch: 等等…
t_en: Hold on...

t_ch: 这段时间，我也算是一步步看着您越变越强了啊！再与我对练一番如何？
t_en: I've been watching your progress during this period! How about another spar?

t_ch: 来吧！
t_en: Bring it on.

t_ch: 来吧！看我这次怎么打败你！
t_en: Bring it on! Let's see how I beat you this time!

t_ch: 来得好！
t_en: That's the spirit!

t_ch: 下次吧。
t_en: Next time.

t_ch: 下次吧，我还有点别的事要办。
t_en: Maybe next time. I've got something else to do now.

t_ch: 没问题！朝九晚五，随时奉陪！
t_en: No problem! I'm ready whenever you need me.

t_ch: 走吧，我带各位去见识见识「竞锋舰」。
t_en: Let's go. I'll give you a tour of the Skysplitter.

t_ch: （前往迴星港）
t_en: (Head to Stargazer Navalia)

t_ch: （稍后再去）
t_en: (Go later)

t_ch: 彦卿知道一个地方视野很好，可以看得比较清楚。走吧。
t_en: I know a spot with a great view of the whole place. Follow me.

t_ch: 没想到来眺望竞锋舰的人还不少啊？
t_en: Looks like a lot of other visitors have also come to catch a glimpse of the Skysplitter.

t_ch: 椒丘先生，怎么了？
t_en: What's up, Mr. Jiaoqiu?

t_ch: 不…没事。
t_en: No... it's nothing.

t_ch: 你们看到了吗，远处那艘飞舰，就是这次演武仪典的比武赛场——「竞锋舰」。
t_en: Do you see that airship in the distance? That's the Skysplitter, the venue for the Wardance ceremony.

t_ch: 远远看来，倒也不算特别。
t_en: It doesn't look all that impressive from this distance.

t_ch: 这艘竞锋舰，是以罗浮退役舰船改造而成的。在演武仪典正式举行前，暂时还不准人们登陆。
t_en: The Skysplitter is actually a decommissioned Luofu military vessel. People aren't allowed on board until the Wardance officially commences.

t_ch: 但明日钟声响起，礼炮绽放，彦卿将代表仙舟「罗浮」云骑军，站上擂台，接受四方骁勇之士的挑战。
t_en: Tomorrow, when the bell rings and the ceremonial cannons roar, I'll be there representing the Cloud Knights of the Xianzhou Luofu, standing in the ring, ready to take on challengers from all over the cosmos.

t_ch: 彦卿自小就被将军带在身边，教授剑术与兵法。每日挥剑斩击一万次，刺击一万次，如是往复，如是往复……
t_en: Since I was a kid, I've been training in swordplay and the art of war under the General. Every day I'd swing my sword ten thousand times, and then thrust it ten thousand times, repeating the process over and over...

t_ch: 我明白，我和一般的孩子似乎不太一样。我从来没有羡慕过他们拥有的玩具和自由，也从没觉得一心练剑是什么枯燥、艰难的事情。
t_en: I understand that I'm not like other kids. I never envied the toys and freedom they all had. I never found sword practice boring or hard.

t_ch: 即便是登上战场，斩阵杀敌，在与那些恶形恶状的孽物交锋时，我也一无所惧。
t_en: Even in the thick of battle, facing down savage abominations, I never felt scared.

t_ch: 每天都能感觉自己在不断变强、变强、再变强一点…一次次将胜利握在手里，世界上没有比这更快乐的事情了。
t_en: Each day, I could feel myself getting stronger and stronger... and I racked up countless victories. It's the best feeling in the world.

t_ch: 但后来，我接了某人一剑。那一剑将我原本完满无缺的自信斩得粉碎。在那一剑到来的瞬间，我第一次感受到了害怕。这也许就是椒丘先生所说的「死生刹那，万念成空」吧。
t_en: But then, I faced a really tough opponent, and just one slash shattered my confidence into a thousand pieces. That's when I felt true fear for the first time. Maybe that's what Mr. Jiaoqiu meant by "life and death hinge on a singular moment, every idea fades into nothingness?"

t_ch: 那之后，彦卿不得不低下头去，将这些碎片一片片拾起、拼合，试图重新拼出过去那个快乐的自己。但无论怎么做，我似乎都无法再现往日的心境了。
t_en: After that, I had to pick up the pieces and try to put myself back together. But no matter how hard I tried, I couldn't seem to find my old, happy self again.

t_ch: 我时不时会问自己，我究竟为何而挥剑？如果注定要面对下一场失败，我又为何要继续挥剑？是为了找回胜利的快乐？为了回应将军的期待？还是为了留下云骑功勋？
t_en: I often ask myself, why do I wield my sword? If defeat is inevitable, why do I continue to fight? Is it to reclaim the joy of victory, to meet the general's expectations, or to secure my honor among the Cloud Knights?

t_ch: 将军能指教我剑术，却不能教我挥剑的理由。他对我说：挥剑的理由，必须由我自己寻得。为此，彦卿百般苦恼，辗转反侧。
t_en: While the general could teach me the art of swordplay, he couldn't teach me why I should keep on going. He said the reason must come from within myself. I've been struggling to find that reason.

t_ch: 但与椒丘先生这番畅谈，彦卿心中已有了答案。
t_en: But after talking with you, Mr. Jiaoqiu, I think I already have that reason in mind.

t_ch: 作为云骑的一员，将军的弟子，我背负了很多，而且注定要背负更多的东西。
t_en: As a member of the Cloud Knights and the general's apprentice, I've had a weight on my shoulders, and I know there's still more to shoulder.

t_ch: 但只有在我挥剑时，我能感觉到自己可以放下一切。我喜欢那个倾尽全力向前方阻碍挥出一剑，一往无前的我。
t_en: But when I wield my sword, it feels like I'm letting go of everything. I love the feeling of giving it my all, facing any obstacle in front of me, pressing forward.

t_ch: 我也正是为了这样的我而挥剑。
t_en: That's why I wield my sword.

t_ch: 彦卿啊彦卿，小小年纪就一把年纪了…说起来你今年到底多大了？
t_en: Oh, Yanqing, so young, yet so grown-up... By the way, how old are you exactly?

t_ch: 这和我几岁没关系。只要是练剑之人，自然会理解我的感受。
t_en: Age doesn't really matter. All swordmasters will understand how I feel.

t_ch: 唉…我明白，罗浮的孩子真是太苦了。那你有什么想说的，云璃小姐？
t_en: Hmm... I get it. Looks like all the kids on the Luofu live tough lives. So, how about you, Miss Yunli?

t_ch: 无论在哪个仙舟上，问女孩子的年龄都是不礼貌的。
t_en: It's not polite to ask a girl her age, no matter which Xianzhou ship you're on.

t_ch: 我问的不是年龄！我是问…你有没有彦卿兄弟这样的梦想？
t_en: I'm not asking your age! I'm asking if you have a dream like Yanqing has?

t_ch: 你不像个厨子，倒是更像主持人。
t_en: You don't talk like a cook, you sound more like a TV host or something.

t_ch: …给我记好了，我是医士。
t_en: ...Need I repeat myself again? I'm a healer.

t_ch: 我…没有彦卿小弟这样的梦想。我之所以要挑战守擂竞锋，只不过是因为答应了爷爷，想赢下他赠给演武仪典的宝剑。
t_en: Well, I... I don't have a dream like Yanqing does. The only reason I'm participating in the Ringmaster's Challenge is because I made a promise to my grandfather. I wanna win the precious sword he's contributed to the Wardance.

t_ch: 你这个人啊，满脑子都是剑。
t_en: Sounds like that mind of yours is just filled with swords.

t_ch: 你脑袋里不也一样没别的吗！
t_en: I bet you've got nothing better on your mind!

t_ch: 我…父亲是朱明仙舟的匠师，只因他的一念愚蠢，许多人死在了他所造的魔剑之下。
t_en: My... father... was a craftsman on the Xianzhou Zhuming. Because of his foolishness, many innocent people fell victim to the cursed swords he forged.

t_ch: 我从小时候起就明白，许多人的手中根本不配持有任何武器。准许他们握剑，就是对无辜之人的残忍。
t_en: Since I was a kid, it's been clear to me that not everyone deserves to have a weapon in their hands. Giving them a sword is no different than being cruel to the innocent.

t_ch: 每当遇见有人德不配剑，便难免手心痒痒，想要从他手里夺下武器。这不是…彦卿小弟要为这次演武仪典守擂嘛。我好心上场，以免宝剑所托非人。
t_en: So, whenever I come across someone unworthy of a sword, I can't help but want to take it away from them. Given that Yanqing is the Wardance ringmaster, I'm stepping up to challenge him, to ensure the precious sword doesn't fall into the hands of an unworthy master.

t_ch: 什么叫所托非人啊，你给我讲讲清楚！
t_en: Hey! What do you mean by "an unworthy master"?

t_ch: 唉，明白了，朱明的孩子也很苦。
t_en: I see. It's not easy for kids on the Zhuming either.

t_ch: 有挥剑的理由，总强过茫然不知所措。我这一生救治过不少云骑，其中也不乏似二位这样出类拔萃的战士……
t_en: Well, it's better to have a reason for wielding a sword than to be lost and confused. I've saved countless Cloud Knights in my life, and there are plenty of exceptional warriors just like the two... of you...

t_ch: 怎么话说到一半，椒丘先生？
t_en: What happened, Mr. Jiaoqiu?

t_ch: …只是想起了几位故人，一些旧事。
t_en: ...Oh, nothing. I was just reminded of some old friends and old tales.

t_ch: 以我身为医士的专业眼光来看，两位的生命力充沛健旺，气息流转如猛火烈风。这场比斗…一定好看的很。
t_en: Judging from my professional perspective as a healer, both of you possess remarkable vitality, your energies flow like raging fires and mighty gales. The upcoming fight... will definitely be impressive.

t_ch: 好了，迴星港兜兜转转一遍，「竞锋舰」也瞧过了。是时候要和各位暂时道别了。
t_en: Well, we've seen the Skysplitter and toured the Stargazer Navalia. I guess it's time to say goodbye for now.

t_ch: 怎么，你要走了吗？你还没问我有没有梦想呢！我也练得很辛苦的！
t_en: What, you're leaving already? But you haven't asked me about my dreams! I've been working hard too, you know!

t_ch: 时候不早了，三月小姐。我和你们几位不同，我只是个受职务羁绊的成年人，我家将军交给我的事情可不会自动完成。
t_en: It's getting late, Miss March. Unlike you lot, I'm a grown-up bound by responsibilities and duties. The tasks entrusted to me by the general won't complete themselves.

t_ch: 对了，彦卿小兄弟，似「迴星港」这般自动运行的区域，平日里也会有咱们这么多人来闲逛吗？
t_en: By the way, Yanqing, is it normal to have so many people wandering around in an automated area like the Stargazer Navalia?

t_ch: ……其实，这儿本是不允许随便擅闯的地方。只是大家是客人，我才带各位来此看看。
t_en: ...Actually, this is a restricted area, but since you're all guests, I made an exception so you could take a look around.

t_ch: 我明白了。那鄙人就先告辞了，祝二位明日擂场，各得所愿。
t_en: I understand. Well, I'll take my leave. I wish you both the best of luck in the ring tomorrow.

t_ch: 椒丘挥了挥手，随即离去。
t_en: Jiaoqiu waves and then leaves...

t_ch: 真是的…我想了半天自己的梦想是什么，他居然不问了！
t_en: Seriously... I just spent so much time thinking about my dream, but he didn't even ask me!

t_ch: 竞锋舰看完了，该回去继续训练了吧？
t_en: Now that we're done with our tour of the Skysplitter, shall we continue with our training?

t_ch: 今天就休息一天吧？
t_en: Why don't we take a day off?

t_ch: 怎么，你想躲起来一个人练剑？想得美。
t_en: What? You want to secretly practice swordplay by yourself? Dream on.

t_ch: 哼，临阵磨枪，一点儿用场也派不上。
t_en: Hmph, you know cramming before a fight never works out.

t_ch: 说来也怪，看到竞锋舰的轮廓，我突然有了信心。所以我打算养精蓄锐，等待明天。
t_en: For some reason, seeing the Skysplitter has boosted my confidence. So, I've decided to conserve my strength for tomorrow.

t_ch: 我带你们离开迴星港吧。
t_en: Alright, I'll take you out of the Stargazer Navalia.

t_ch: …闭嘴吧赤牙，这里的船毕竟不是兽舰，我需要点时间搞定它。
t_en: ...Oh, just shut up, Red Fang! This is not a beast ship, I need some time to take care of things.

t_ch: 你自愿披上了贱畜的皮，加入这次行动，为光荣的大业献身。现在你告诉我，你搞不定？你知道我们需要多少条船吗？
t_en: You willingly donned the skin of a lowly beast to join this mission, dedicating yourself to our glorious cause. And now you're telling me you can't handle it? Do you realize how many ships we need?

t_ch: 我在尽力，我在研究，这些都需要时间。
t_en: I'm doing my best, alright? It takes time to figure all this out.

t_ch: 明天礼炮一响，所有人的注意都会被吸引，不会有第二次机会——
t_en: When the guns go off tomorrow, all eyes will be on it. That'll be our only chance...

t_ch: Harr zaa xi？谁，谁在那儿？！
t_en: Harr-zaa-xi? Wh—Who's there!?

t_ch: 你们是谁？
t_en: Who are you guys?

t_ch: 临时检查。为什么有无关的人在迴星港出没？还是几个小不点。
t_en: An impromptu inspection. Why are there outsiders loitering in Stargazer Navalia? And a bunch of kids at that!

t_ch: 小朋友，你们的父母难道没告诉过你，迴星港虽然是自动运转的设施，但这并不意味着你们几个能随随便便闯进来。
t_en: Hey, kids, didn't your parents ever tell you to stay away from the Stargazer Navalia? I know it's an automated facility, but it doesn't mean you can just break in and do what you want.

t_ch: 我这就带弟弟妹妹们走。
t_en: I'll take the little ones away right now.

t_ch: 啊，对不住，我这就把小不点们带走。
t_en: Ah, sorry, I'll take the little ones away right now.

t_ch: 叫谁小朋友呐。
t_en: Who are you calling "kids?"

t_ch: 首先，本小姐是成年人了。其次，我可不算随随便便闯进来的——
t_en: First of all, I'm an adult. Second, I didn't just break in...

t_ch: 是啦，叔叔，咱们是坐星槎呼一下飞过来的喔！
t_en: Yeah, we flew here on a starskiff! Like... woosh!

t_ch: 叔叔们没有责问你们的意思。但是，这儿不对一般居民们开放。
t_en: Well, I'm not trying to tell you off, but this place is off-limits to the public, you know?

t_ch: 姐姐，姐姐，咱们快走吧，接下来我要、我要去永狩原玩！
t_en: Big sis! Let's go! I want to play in Ever-Hunt Plains!

t_ch: 唉？永狩原？呃…好，好的。姐姐带你去永狩原。
t_en: Huh? Ever-Hunt Plains? Uh... yeah, sure! Big sis will take you there.

t_ch: Xuu haa…你应该让我——
t_en: Xuu haa... You should have let me...

t_ch: 嘘，检修结束了，一切正常，我们该走了。
t_en: Shhh, the overhaul is done, and everything looks good. We should leave.

t_ch: 事情…似乎有点蹊跷。换个地方说话。
t_en: Something smells fishy... Let's talk somewhere else.

t_ch: 彦卿小弟，能不能把刚才的话再学一遍？
t_en: Could you repeat what you just said, Yanqing?

t_ch: 什么话？
t_en: What did I say?

t_ch: 姐姐，姐姐，咱们快走吧~
t_en: Big sis! Let's go! I want to play in Ever-Hunt Plains!

t_ch: 救命，你是永远读不懂气氛吗？刚才那三个人，很有问题。
t_en: Come on. Can't you read the room? Something is definitely off about the three people we just met.

t_ch: 傻瓜都看明白啦，我只是想再听你说一遍那个。
t_en: Yeah, anyone could see that. I just wanted to hear you say it again.

t_ch: 那只粉毛狐狸刚刚欲言又止，怕是早已瞧出了什么端倪。只是他人生地不熟，所以临走前了留了个话茬暗示咱们，没想到你却不搭理他。
t_en: That pink-haired fox tried to say something. I'm pretty sure he sensed something fishy. Since he's not familiar with this place, he just dropped us a hint, but you didn't seem to be paying attention at all.

t_ch: 我早就听明白了！
t_en: I knew that from the beginning!

t_ch: 那个云骑…不认识彦卿师父？
t_en: That Cloud Knight... didn't recognize Master Yanqing?

t_ch: 刚才那个云骑，完全认不出彦卿师父…这确实很蹊跷。
t_en: That Cloud Knight didn't recognize Master Yanqing at all... That's really weird.

t_ch: 怎么，他在罗浮很有名吗？朱明云骑军千千万万，就算听过我的大名，也不见得认得出我的脸。
t_en: What, is he famous on the Luofu or something? Not even the Cloud Knights on the Zhuming, who all know my glorious name, might recognize my face.

t_ch: 您说的是呐。
t_en: You have a point.

t_ch: 他们似乎在捣鼓星槎制造线？
t_en: Looks like they were tampering with the starskiff production line, huh?

t_ch: 说是检修，但看起来他们似乎并不知道怎么摆弄那条星槎制造的流水线？
t_en: They said they were doing an overhaul, but it looked like they had no idea how to operate the starskiff production line, right?

t_ch: 这几个人一见到咱们就立刻「检修完毕」，转身就走，实在是可疑。
t_en: And it's suspicious how they suddenly finished their "overhaul" and walked away as soon as they laid eyes on us.

t_ch: 云骑，天舶司的人，还有匠人…种类倒是齐全，检修的理由也说得过去，只是刚才那人嘴里迸出了一句奇怪的语言。你们听见了吗？
t_en: A Cloud Knight, a member from the Sky-Faring Commission, and a craftsman... They're from various departments, and the reason for the overhaul seems legit, but one of them blurted out some weird language just now. Did you hear that?

t_ch: 我有种奇怪的直觉，如果咱们现在悄悄跟上去，一定能掀开这几个家伙的马脚。
t_en: I have a feeling that if we secretly tail them, we'll definitely catch these guys in the act.

t_ch: 跟上我，小心别让他们发现了。
t_en: Follow my lead, and be careful not to blow our cover.

t_ch: 三月小姐，你要往哪儿去啊？跟着他们！
t_en: Where are you going, Miss March? Follow them!

t_ch: 脚步要轻，呼吸要浅…
t_en: Tread softly, breathe quietly...

t_ch: 还要随时留意对方的行动。一有动静就躲起来。
t_en: And make sure to keep an eye on them. Hide out of sight as soon as there's any sign of activity.

t_ch: 三月小姐，别靠太近了，会被发现的！
t_en: Don't get too close, Miss March! They might spot you!

t_ch: 拍摄可疑人士密谋现场
t_en: Take a photo of the suspects plotting at the scene

t_ch: 以<unbreak>4.0</unbreak>倍率拍摄到清晰证据
t_en: Take a clear photo of the evidence using <unbreak>4.0</unbreak>× zoom.

t_ch: 可疑的云骑士卒
t_en: Suspicious Cloud Knight Soldier

t_ch: 可疑的狐人甲
t_en: Suspicious Foxian A

t_ch: 可疑的狐人乙
t_en: Suspicious Foxian B

t_ch: 只要离港，便有猎群的人接应…
t_en: Someone from the pack will be waiting for me once I leave the port...

t_ch: 末度大人已经准备妥当了…
t_en: Lord Mok Tok has already prepared everything...

t_ch: 以<unbreak>3.0</unbreak>倍率拍摄到清晰证据
t_en: Take a clear photo of the evidence using <unbreak>3.0</unbreak>× zoom.

t_ch: 可疑的狐人伸手瞬间
t_en: The moment the suspicious foxian reaches out.

t_ch: 可疑的狐人面部
t_en: Face of the suspicious foxian.

t_ch: 拍摄可疑人士密谋现场
t_en: Take a photo of the suspects plotting at the scene

t_ch: 可疑的狐人
t_en: Suspicious Foxian

t_ch: 应该宰了刚才那三只贱畜的。
t_en: We should have just killed those lowly beasts.

t_ch: 小崽子占不了多大地方。这儿到处都是箱子，随便往哪个里头一扔，不会有人发现的。
t_en: Those little brats won't take up much space. There are boxes all over this place. Just dump them into one and no one will notice.

t_ch: 少给我节外生枝，伽虏。任何一点意外都会干扰末度大人的计划。
t_en: Cut the theatrics, Grulok. Even the slightest slip-up could interfere with Lord Mok Tok's plans.

t_ch: 接下来去哪？
t_en: So, where are we heading next?

t_ch: 去查验货船，需要做的准备还有很多。还有，记得带走那几只箱子。
t_en: To check the freight skiffs. We've got a lot of preparations to do. Also, don't forget to take those crates with you.

t_ch: 武器、补给…我们必须做好充足的准备。不然咱们的头颅就只能交代在这儿了。
t_en: Weapons, supplies... we've got to be well-prepared. Otherwise, we're screwed.

t_ch: 这些人…是走私者吗？他们要做什么？
t_en: So, are they... smugglers? What exactly are they up to?

t_ch: 我不知道。但他们似乎准备带走那堆货箱。我有个主意，我们可以躲进那些箱子里，跟着他们。
t_en: I have no clue, but they seem to be moving those crates. I've got an idea. We can hide inside the crates and follow them.

t_ch: 趁着几人暂时离去，你如履薄冰地来到一个箱子旁，钻了进去。
t_en: Taking advantage of the temporary absence of the group, you cautiously approach a crate and crawl inside.

t_ch: 好窄…
t_en: Ugh, it's so cramped in here...

t_ch: 要窒息了。
t_en: I can hardly breathe.

t_ch: 坚持住…
t_en: Just hang in there...

t_ch: 你们听到箱子旁有脚步声不断来回。如果在此时有人打开货箱，一定能瞧见你们可笑的躲藏姿态。万幸，事情没有发生。
t_en: You hear footsteps pacing back and forth beside the crate. If someone were to open the crate at that moment, they would certainly catch you in your ridiculous hiding position. Fortunately, that doesn't happen.

t_ch: 箱底的悬浮机关被启动。你们感到它开始向着前方缓缓飘行。
t_en: The floating mechanism at the bottom of the crate is activated. You feel it begins to drift slowly forward.

t_ch: 趁着这几名可疑的「官方人员」暂时离去，你如履薄冰地来到一个箱子旁，试图钻进去。
t_en: Seizing the moment when the suspicious "official personnel" temporarily step away, you tread on thin ice to reach a crate, attempting to slip inside.

t_ch: 很遗憾，箱子里塞满了各色小巧的机关装置。
t_en: Unfortunately, the crate is crammed with an assortment of petite, intricate mechanism devices.

t_ch: 不行，进不去…得换个箱子。
t_en: Nope, won't fit... Better try another.

t_ch: 长如一个琥珀纪的闭气和沉默后，你们听到箱子落地声。
t_en: After what feels like an entire Amber Era of holding breath in silence, you heard the sound of the crate touching down.

t_ch: 货先放在这儿吧？接下来检查船只的问题。
t_en: Let's just put the cargo here for now, alright? Then we'll move on to inspect the ships.

t_ch: 末度大人说，一旦接应完成，弟兄们搭上货船离开这儿。
t_en: Lord Mok Tok said that as soon as we're done, we're to board the freight skiff and leave this place.

t_ch: 放心吧，我改写了舵航计划表。你们俩，跟我来。
t_en: Don't worry. I've changed the shipping schedule. You two, come with me.

t_ch: 我总觉得奇怪，贱畜的味道怎么哪儿都有。
t_en: Is it just me? I keep smelling that stench of lowly beasts everywhere we go.

t_ch: 别疑神疑鬼。
t_en: Don't be so paranoid.

t_ch: 离去的脚步声仿佛一个信号，让你们松了口气。
t_en: The departing footsteps sound like a signal, prompting you to breathe a sigh of relief.

t_ch: 看来他们打算利用迴星港的船只，带他们离开仙舟。
t_en: Looks like they're planning to escape on the skiffs in Stargazer Navalia.

t_ch: 口口声声计划计划，这些人到底是从哪儿来的？他们想在仙舟上干什么？
t_en: They're keep talking about their "plans," but where did they come from? And what do they want to do on the Xianzhou?

t_ch: 八成不是什么好事。他们不见了？咱们跟上去！
t_en: They're definitely up to something bad. Wait, they disappeared? Let's catch up to them!

t_ch: 不对味，有奇怪的味道…
t_en: Something doesn't smell right. There's a weird scent...
